Special Correspondence to The Press


WASHINGTON, D. c., June TO-
Uncle Sans experiment as a finder
of jobs for the immigrant has
proven the most flattering kind of
a success. That is, as an expert
ment.


Since January he has introduced
more than 600 jobless immigrants
to manless Jobs in 81 states.


This record is considered good, in
view of the fact railroads and fac
tories have been overstocked with
labor since the first Of the year
in fact laying off men. And the
scheme is only in it's infancy.


This Observation-Disclosures
migrant" idea is something new.
The work is being done through
what is known as the division Of
information. If you wanted to you
could call 1t the government en-
ployment bureau. It works just like
an employment bureau, except that
two of the distinctive features of
the average employment agency
are missing. Uncle Sam makes no
charges for his services. Nor does
he rob his clients out of the last
few dollars they possess, leaving
them stranded and jobless in a
strange world.


The main Office of the division
Of information" is in Washington,
in charge of Terrence V. Powderly.
The branch Office in the Maritime
building in New York is where the
immigrant applies for work, and
where he gets it.


While it is not necessary to be
an immigrant to get position,
there is no use writing to the
Washington Office for work. For
the present, while the division of
information is still in it's infancy,
it S almost necessary to apply at
the New York Office personally.
You could write to the New York
office, explaining just what kind Of
